mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of declining rate periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to generate positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of rising INDPRO observations within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer confidence and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic activity, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite the production decline.
